narrative.headline: Operate a Shopify store from the terminal with curated Admin GraphQL commands, local sync, analytics, and bulk exports.
narrative.value_prop: Endpoint mirrors for orders, products, customers, inventory items, and fulfillment orders. A local SQLite store for offline reads and full-text search after sync. MCP server with both stdio and HTTP transport so agents (OpenAI Codex CLI, hosted clients) consume the same surface without learning GraphQL.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN to a custom-app token with the read scopes you need (read_orders, read_products, read_customers, read_inventory, read_fulfillments).

quickstart.comment: Local archive state for all resources before any sync.
List recent orders with agent-native JSON.
Inspect the current Shopify bulk export job.

troubleshoots.fix: Verify SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN is an Admin API token, not a Storefront API token. Re-issue under custom-app settings.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from local search or analytics
troubleshoots.fix: Run shopify-pp-cli sync --full first; analytics and search read from the local SQLite store.
Run shopify-pp-cli workflow status --json to see per-resource state, then sync the specific resource that is stale.

narrative.when_to_use: Use when an agent needs Shopify Admin GraphQL data without learning GraphQL. The MCP server exposes both stdio (Codex CLI) and HTTP (hosted agents). Best for read-heavy ops workflows and as the MCP backend for agent-driven Shopify operations.

recipes.title: Confirm a clean install
recipes.explanation: JSON health output suitable for piping into agent context.

recipes.title: Inspect local archive state
recipes.explanation: Per-resource sync state, row counts, age — agent's freshness oracle before running an offline query.

recipes.title: Narrow a verbose response for agents
recipes.explanation: Pairs --agent with --select dotted paths so agents do not burn context on fields they did not ask for.

recipes.title: Check a bulk export status
recipes.explanation: Bulk operations are async; this returns the current or most recent job state in one MCP call.
